title: DC Circuit Builder
libraryReference: Series Circuit Builder; Parallel Circuit Builder
summary: Learners assemble battery-fed DC circuits, compare series and parallel behavior, and observe how voltage, current, and power change as loads are added or removed.
learningObjectives: Compare voltage and current behavior in series and parallel DC circuits.
Calculate equivalent resistance for simple branch arrangements.
Connect circuit layout decisions to load performance and power use.

environment.setting: Workbench with battery bank, resistive loads, lamps, and branch wiring rails
environment.focus: Hands-on DC reasoning with immediate circuit feedback

interactables.label: DC source pack
interactables.teaches: Provides a stable source for comparing circuit arrangements.

interactables.label: Series wiring rail
interactables.teaches: Shows one current path with voltage drop across multiple loads.

interactables.label: Parallel wiring rail
interactables.teaches: Shows equal branch voltage with divided current paths.

interactables.label: Watts and amps display
interactables.teaches: Links resistance changes to total current draw and power.

tasks: Build a working series circuit with two lamps and record the voltage drop across each load.
Rebuild the circuit in parallel and compare branch current behavior.
Increase branch count until the source current target is reached.
Explain which layout is better for independent load operation and why.
completion: Learner builds both series and parallel circuits correctly.
Learner identifies the correct equivalent resistance trend.
Learner explains the DC behavior of each layout.
